title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Inference Brief: Envelope Identity Causality
summary: Consumers parse Buffer content directly into trusted domain types, overload routing keys with identity data, lack stable event IDs and versions, and break when producers add or remove fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the envelope identity causality increment in versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Envelope Identity Causality · Theory Envelope Identity Causality
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 4741 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
